name: Courgette'meatballs'
recipeIngredient: 1 fresh hot green chili, finely chopped
5 tbsp vegetable oil for deep frying
2 medium onions, peeled and finely chopped
1/2 lb (about 2 tomatoes) peeled and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash, trim, and grate the courgettes. Place them in a bowl and sprinkle with salt. Set aside for 30 minutes.
2. Squeeze as much liquid as possible from the courgettes by pressing handfuls between your palms. Save this liquid for the sauce. Dry off the bowl and put the courgetes back in it.
3. Add chopped chili, onion, ginger, and coriander to the vegetable mixture. Sift graham flour over the mixture and mix well. Form 16 neat balls by throwing them from one hand to another.
4. In a frying pan, wok, or other deep-frying utensil, heat about 1/2 inch (1/4 cm) of oil over medium flame. When hot, add 5-6 meatballs at a time, frying for about 1.5 minutes, or until they turn rich reddish-brown.
5. Turn the balls occasionally as they fry. Remove with a slotted spoon when done and drain on a plate lined with kitchen paper. Repeat this process for all meatballs.
6. To make the sauce, remove 5 tbsp of oil used in deep frying and put it in a 10" (25cm) frying or sauté pan. Heat over medium flame.
7. When hot, add onions and stir-fry for 7-8 minutes or until they begin to turn brown at the edges. Turn off heat briefly, then add turmeric, cayenne, ground cumin, and ground coriander. Stir once and return to fire.
8. Add chopped tomatoes and stir fry on medium heat for 5 minutes. Add courgette juice (8 fl), if needed with water. Bring to boil. Lower heat and let the sauce simmer for 15 minutes.
9. Add cream, garam masala, ground roasted cumin, and salt. Mix well, bring to simmer and cook for 1 minute.
10. Add meatballs and spoon sauce over them. Cover and gently simmer for 6-7 minutes. Spoon sauce over meatballs a few times during cooking period.
11. These meatballs become very soft when cooked in the sauce; handle gently and serve immediately.
Makes 16 meatballs, serving 4 (normal) - 6 (feather-weight).
